assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.
Implement a module of a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder that uses the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture.
S[32:1]: 32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.
The top module adder_32bit consists of several instances of the 16 bit CLA block you design.

expected_properties: C32 asserted when the 32‑bit addition overflows

cases.goal: Validate correct carry‑out behavior on overflow condition
notes: Uses maximal operand values (e.g., A = 0xFFFFFFFF, B = 0xFFFFFFFF) to provoke a carry out.

expected_properties: Each new input pair yields the correct sum independent of previous stimulus
cases.goal: Check combinational independence across successive stimulus applications
notes: Apply two distinct input vectors sequentially without inserting explicit delays.
stimulus_intent: Apply first vector then second vector
timing_assumptions: No timing assumptions beyond combinational stabilization between vectors.
